deep_dive.title: MIRROR: Aligning Semantic Relations from Language to Image via Gromov--Wasserstein
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) inherit rich relational priors from their language backbones, yet often fail when asked to apply these relationships in visual contexts. We trace this failure to a structural blind spot: projection-based alignment trains each visual token to carry the right semantics, but never asks whether the relationships between concepts survive the crossing from language to vision. To address this, we propose MIRROR (Mapping Inter-concept Relations from language to visual Representation via Optimal-transport-based Regularization), a geometric regularization framework that transfers relational priors from language to vision by exploiting the rich relational structure encoded in language representations. Specifically, we derive a surrogate loss from the proposed Semi-Inverse Gromov-Wasserstein (SI-GW) problem, an inverse geometric problem that aligns visual representations with language-derived relational priors. We show that this formulation admits a unique closed-form solution that prescribes the ideal visual relational structure implied by language geometry and cross-modal coupling. The structure of the formulation also enables efficient computation, making it applicable to long token sequences. Applying SI-GW inside decoder-only Transformers requires careful design. We introduce targeted strategies at the layer, head, and token levels to ensure stable extraction without additional parameters or inference cost. MIRROR improves relational consistency while preserving performance on general vision-language tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: aligns relational priors from language to vision in MLLMs via optimal transport regularization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过最优传输正则化将语言关系先验对齐到视觉，改进多模态大模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过最优传输正则化将语言关系先验对齐到视觉，改进多模态大模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过最优传输正则化将语言关系先验对齐到视觉，改进多模态大模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses MLLMs failure in applying relationships visually by transferring relational priors from language to vision using Gromov-Wasserstein optimal transport, improving cross-modal alignment.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型继承语言关系先验，但视觉场景中常失败。本文提出MIRROR，利用Gromov-Wasserstein最优传输将语言关系先验正则化地迁移到视觉表示，从而增强模型的关系推理能力。该框架具有通用性，可广泛集成到现有多模态模型中，为缩小语言与视觉的语义鸿沟提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Text Over Image: Auditing Multimodal Robustness in Synthetic Medical Image Detection
deep_dive.abstract: With the rapid adoption of generative AI, synthetic medical images pose growing risks, including diagnostic deception and insurance fraud. Although prior work has explored vision-language model (VLM)-based synthetic image detection, these evaluations typically consider images in isolation. In clinical practice, however, images are interpreted alongside structured records and metadata, and VLMs are increasingly deployed under joint image-record inputs. We uncover a previously underexamined multimodal vulnerability: when given both modalities, VLMs may overweight record context in authenticity judgments, such that the same image receives different predictions solely due to changes in its accompanying text. This raises concerns about robustness in real-world deployment. To systematically characterize this effect, we reformulate synthetic medical image detection as an audit of multimodal robustness at the image-record interface and introduce a paired benchmark that holds the image fixed while swapping controlled metadata variants. Across multiple imaging modalities, we evaluate diverse open-weight and frontier API VLMs and find that changing the metadata context alone can flip authenticity judgments, with accuracy on authentic images dropping by 61.1% on average under an explicit AI-origin tag. We further propose an inference-time mitigation pipeline that detects and neutralizes provenance shortcuts without model retraining, substantially outperforming direct prompt-based suppression on the affected subset. Our benchmark provides a standardized tool for assessing and improving multimodal robustness beyond image-only settings. Code and data will be released upon acceptance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: studies VLM vulnerability to text context in synthetic medical image detection
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究视觉语言模型在合成医学图像检测中受文本上下文影响的脆弱性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究视觉语言模型在合成医学图像检测中受文本上下文影响的脆弱性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究视觉语言模型在合成医学图像检测中受文本上下文影响的脆弱性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Reveals that VLMs may overweight record context when judging authenticity of medical images, causing inconsistent predictions, and proposes evaluation methods for this multimodal robustness issue.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 发现视觉语言模型在联合图像与文本记录进行合成医学图像检测时，会过度依赖记录上下文，导致同一图像因文本不同而得出不同真实性判断。本文揭示了这一多模态脆弱性，并提供了审计方法，强调需在多模态输入下审慎评估模型鲁棒性，为安全敏感的医疗诊断提供警示。

deep_dive.title: Mural: Transferring LLM knowledge to image generation via Mixture-of-Transformers
deep_dive.abstract: Leveraging capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in text-to-image (T2I) synthesis is an important research direction. In this work we investigate whether the knowledge of a frozen LLM can be effectively utilized in T2I generation when trained exclusively on standard text-image pairs. We integrate a frozen, reasoning-capable LLM with a diffusion-based image generator via shared attention within the Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture. Our experiments span two critical questions: (1) what degree of the LLM's intrinsic knowledge remains accessible during T2I training, and (2) what novel capabilities emerge in the resulting system. Across established benchmarks, our models achieve strong performance among unified understanding-generation systems: 0.85 on GenEval, 86.75 on DPG-Bench, and 0.66 on WISE with inference-time reasoning, using only text-image data. Remarkably, we uncover emergent behaviors absent from training data, including cross-lingual image generation, color-guided composition, emoji / ASCII scene construction, and generation directed by world knowledge. These results demonstrate that pretrained LLM knowledge can guide image synthesis under standard text-to-image training paradigms, without interleaved multimodal signals or explicit reasoning supervision. Our findings open new avenues for harnessing frozen model capabilities in resource-constrained multimodal learning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: transfers frozen LLM knowledge to image generation via mixture-of-transformers
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过混合Transformer将冻结的LLM知识迁移到图像生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过混合Transformer将冻结的LLM知识迁移到图像生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过混合Transformer将冻结的LLM知识迁移到图像生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Integrates a frozen reasoning-capable LLM with a diffusion image generator using shared attention in MoT architecture, achieving strong performance in unified understanding-generation tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 探索如何将冻结的大语言模型知识用于文本到图像生成，通过混合Transformer共享注意力机制，将推理能力融入扩散模型，在理解与生成统一任务上取得优异性能。该方法表明，即使不微调，LLM的丰富语义和推理知识也能显著提升生成质量与一致性，为多模态模型的知识迁移开辟了新路径。

quick_skim.title: MS-Resampler: Multi-Scope Visual Resampling for Efficient Multimodal LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) typically employ resampling-based projectors to transform dense visual features into a compact token sequence for language modeling. Most existing resamplers adopt a single, fixed aggregation scope via global cross-attention, which can blur fine-grained local evidence and limit the ability to capture both local details and global context within a fixed token budget. In this work, we propose MS-Resampler, a multi-scope visual resampling framework for MLLMs. MS-Resampler instantiates multiple scope-specific resamplers by injecting explicit spatial scope priors into the resampling attention, enabling each branch to aggregate visual information at a particular granularity from local to global. The outputs of these scope-specific resamplers are then adaptively fused to produce the final visual representations for language modeling. Extensive experiments on ten public multimodal benchmarks show that MS-Resampler consistently improves visual understanding and multimodal reasoning over conventional single-scope resamplers, while introducing only minimal computational overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improves multimodal LLMs with multi-scope visual resampling, directly supporting image-and-text processing models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过多范围视觉重采样增强多模态大语言模型，直接支持图像文本处理模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过多范围视觉重采样增强多模态大语言模型，直接支持图像文本处理模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过多范围视觉重采样增强多模态大语言模型，直接支持图像文本处理模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MS-Resampler improves multimodal LLMs by injecting spatial scope priors into resampling attention, allowing multi-granularity visual token aggregation that preserves fine details and context within a fixed budget, enhancing vision-language understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: MS-Resampler通过注入显式空间范围先验，实例化多个特定范围的重采样器，从而在固定令牌预算下更有效地捕捉局部细节和全局上下文，提升多模态大语言模型的视觉理解效率。实验表明该方法能增强视觉表征能力，对图像文本处理任务有广泛益处。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: MS-Resampler通过注入显式空间范围先验，实例化多个特定范围的重采样器，从而在固定令牌预算下更有效地捕捉局部细节和全局上下文，提升多模态大语言模型的视觉理解效率。实验表明该方法能增强视觉表征能力，对图像文本处理任务有广泛益处。
